content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 27, 1990
DonaId Il. zinring
Assistant Superintendent
Las Virgenes Unified school District
30961 West Agoura Road, Suite 100
westlake Vi1lage, CA 913 61
Re: Your Request For Advice
our FiIe No. A-90-167
Dear Mr. zinring:
We have received your request for advice under the conflict
of interest provisions'of the Political 
Reform Act.1/
OUESTION
The Las Virgenes Unified School District (the District) is
principatly located in Los Angel'es county.. Five years ago, trre
bi=trilt 
annexed a snall portion of venturh county. The District
presently has its conflict of interest code fi.ling requirements
with l,os-engeles county. You are now asking if the Distrj-ct can
continue its reporting practice to Los Angeles county.
CONCLUS TON
Since the District 
now has jurisdiction in nore than one
county, the Fair Politicaf Practices Commi.ssion is the appropriate
code ieviewing body, and as such, must approve the Di.strict's
conflict of interest code and provide for the p1ace of fiting for
the statements of economic interest.
L/ covernnent code Sections 81000-91015. Al,l, statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulat.ions
Section l-80oo, et ses. A11 references to regulations are to TitLe
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4.O8O7 . (916) 3 22-5660


Page Two
March 27′  1990
The District will need to
interest code. we have given
identify specific problems and
under a separate letter.
anend to its existj-ng conflict of
the code a cursory review but ''ri11
concerns which need to be corrected
9IE@
The Las Virgenes Unified school District has been principally
located in Los Angeles county. About five years ago a snall
portion of area in neighboring Ventura county was annexed with the
bistrict 
to accommodate students residing in the Bell canyon area.
The BeIl canyon area was originally part of the Simi Unified
School District, 
but a mountain range physically divided BeII
canyon students from the sini Unified school District. 
Residents
in Ltre BelI canyon area successfully petitioned for the annexation
to the Las virgenes unified School- District to alleviate the Iong
and difficult 
commute for students to attend school.
The District's 
enptoyees have been filing their statements of
economic interests under a code developed by the County of Los
Angeles, The District seeks to continue the existing practice of
reporting to the county rather than to the Fair Political
Practices Commission.
ANALYSIS
The Political 
Reform Act requires every agency to adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code. A code designates the
positi6ns within the agency who are in degisionmaking capacities
and further sets out what types of financial interests must be
disclosed by emptoyees in ttrose designated positions. 
(Section
87300-87302.) AII- conflict of interest codes, and any arnendments
to it, must be approved by the appropriate code reviewinqr body.
(Section 87303.) The code reviewing body for any agency with
jurisdiction in more than one county is the Fair Political
Practices Commission. (Section 82011. )
There is nothing in the Political Reforn Act, or its
inplementing regulations, which excepts from the definition of a
rnulti-county agency an agency with boundaries that cross county
lines if only a very small portion of the jurisdiction, 
or
property held, is in the neighboring or second county- The
Conlnission has no statutory authority to create this type of
exception. Accordingly, the Las virgenes Unified Schoo1 District
is a local government agency with jurisdiction in rnore than one
county and the appropriate code reviewing body is the Fair
PoIiticaI Practices Cornrnission.


Page Three
March 27′  1990
by February
your annual
Since the code provides that annual statements must be filed
of each year, this change $/i11 not have any effect on
statements for this year. Should any designated
employees assume or
please retain their
with your District.
leave office during this transition period,
completed statements of economic interests
If you have any questions, or wish to discuss thj,s, please
feel- free to contact me at (916) 322-590]-.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
'.' 
: i |"" ' t
By: Jeanette E. Turvill
PoLitical Reform consultant
Legal Division
